articleTitle: Marginal fit and internal adaptation of zirconia complete coverage fixed dental prostheses fabricated by milling versus 3D printing: A systematic review and meta-analysis of in vitro studies.
takeaways: This study analyzed the accuracy of zirconia dental crowns and bridges made through two digital manufacturing methods - milling versus 3D printing - to determine which provides better fit and adaptation to teeth

The research specifically measured marginal fit (how well the crown edges seal against the tooth) and internal adaptation (how well the crown interior matches the tooth shape), both critical factors that determine how long dental restorations last

Previous in vitro studies showed inconsistent results when comparing milling versus 3D printing for zirconia prostheses, requiring this systematic review to combine data from multiple studies for more reliable conclusions

The study focused exclusively on zirconia complete coverage fixed dental prostheses (crowns and bridges that completely cover teeth), which are popular due to zirconia's strength and tooth-like appearance

This meta-analysis provides dentists and patients with evidence-based data to make informed decisions about which digital fabrication method may offer superior precision for zirconia dental restorations
